voice.description: Voice assistant: wake word, your Whisper key, and (admin) speech provider configuration.

wakeWord.title: Wake word
wakeWord.description: Make the voice assistant respond only after you call it by name. Whisper still transcribes what the mic picks up, but the conversation pipeline ignores anything that does not start with the wake word.
wakeWord.hint: After you say the name once, the next questions in the same conversation go through without it until you pause for ~30 s or close the dialog.

voiceMode.apiKeyHint: Stored encrypted in your user settings. Used only for voice-mode transcription.

simpleMode.title: Simple mode
simpleMode.description: Hide advanced controls (RAG strategy, orchestrator, agentic routing, RAG tracing). The chat input and citations remain. Enable for a calmer interface; disable to surface every knob.

agentProfile.title: Knowledge agent profile
agentProfile.description: Bias every chat toward a domain — citation style, tone, evidence hierarchy. Pick None to keep the default behavior.

responseLength.title: Response length
responseLength.description: How long should AI answers be by default. Affects prompt instructions and max tokens.

responseStyle.title: Response style
responseStyle.description: How AI answers should feel — tone and the domain to frame examples in. Length is set above.
responseStyle.neutralHint: Clear, factual, neither casual nor stiff.
responseStyle.academicHint: Precise terminology, formal phrasing, hedge claims.
responseStyle.domainFocusPlaceholder: e.g. machine learning, Croatian legal practice
responseStyle.domainFocusHint: Frames examples and analogies in this domain when possible. Optional.

messageNotifications.toastDescription: Show a popup in the top-right when you get a message from an admin. Turn off to receive it quietly in the bell only.
messageNotifications.soundDescription: Play a sound when a new message arrives.

systemAgent.title: System AI Agent Provider
systemAgent.description: Configure the LLM provider used by internal AI agents (strategy routing, research planning, synthesis). Admin only.
systemAgent.info: This setting controls which LLM is used by all internal Pydantic AI agents. Changes take effect on the next request. The user's chat model selection is separate.

synthesis.title: Synthesis / answer model (optional)
synthesis.description: A separate model that writes the user-facing answer and the model-insight reflection. The agent model above keeps tool-calling reliable; this one can be a stronger or thinking model (e.g. DeepSeek V4-Flash).
synthesis.modelHint: Leave the model name empty to use the agent model for answers too.

systemAgent.saveSuccessDescription: The system agent LLM configuration has been updated. Changes take effect on the next agent request.
systemAgent.saveError: Failed to save system agent configuration.

speech.title: Speech Services
speech.info: STT enables voice input in chat. TTS enables text-to-speech for messages and document reading.

refund.confirmDescription: Your latest payment will be refunded, the subscription cancelled immediately, and your account reverted to the free Researcher plan.

adminOnly.title: Administrator Access Required
adminOnly.description: The settings in this section can only be modified by an administrator. Please contact your system administrator if you require changes.
adminOnly.explainLocalAI: The Local AI tab allows configuration of self-hosted models and the local inference server. This includes managing model paths, service ports, and other backend settings critical to the Local AI functionality.
adminOnly.explainService: The Service tab allows configuration of the core backend service, including network settings, service endpoints, and log management. These are critical system-wide settings.
adminOnly.explainUsers: The Users tab allows administrators to view and manage all registered users in the system, including their roles, settings, and access permissions.

chatTitle.title: Auto-generate chat titles
chatTitle.description: When enabled, new chat titles will be automatically generated. Choose which type of model to use for title generation.

citationStyle.title: Citation Style
citationStyle.description: Default citation format for notes and bibliography.

reasoningIndicators.title: Show reasoning model indicators
reasoningIndicators.description: Shows indicators for models with reasoning capabilities (Claude, GPT-4, DeepSeek, etc.). These models can provide step-by-step thinking in responses.
reasoningIndicators.reasoningDescription: Reasoning models like Claude Sonnet, Qwen and DeepSeek display their thinking process in real-time. Enable the reasoning toggle for models that support step-by-step thinking.

RAGSimilaritySearch.label: Similarity Search
RAGSimilaritySearch.description: Best for: Simple factual lookups and direct question-answer scenarios. • Fast vector similarity search with minimal preprocessing • Single-pass retrieval optimized for speed • Ideal when query terms closely match document vocabulary

RAGSelfQuery.label: Self-Query RAG
RAGSelfQuery.description: Best for: Queries with implicit filters like dates, authors, or document types. • Extracts structured metadata filters from natural language • Combines semantic search with precise metadata filtering • Handles queries like 'papers from 2023 about AI' automatically

RAGHybridSelfQuery.label: Hybrid Self-Query RAG
RAGHybridSelfQuery.description: Best for: Complex queries needing both semantic understanding and exact filters. • Merges self-query metadata extraction with hybrid search • Balances dense vector search and sparse keyword matching • Optimal for structured documents with rich metadata

RAGHyDE.label: HyDE - Hypothetical Document Embeddings
RAGHyDE.description: Best for: Questions where answers use different vocabulary than queries. • Generates hypothetical answer documents using LLM • Searches using answer embeddings instead of query embeddings • Bridges vocabulary gap between questions and technical documents

RAGMultiQuery.label: Multi-Query RAG
RAGMultiQuery.description: Best for: Overcoming vocabulary mismatch and improving recall. • Generates 5 alternative phrasings of the same question • Retrieves documents for each variation in parallel • Combines and deduplicates results with reranking

RAGFusion.label: Fusion RAG
RAGFusion.description: Best for: Comprehensive search requiring diverse retrieval methods. • Uses Reciprocal Rank Fusion or Borda Count for result merging • Combines multiple query variations with advanced fusion algorithms • Maximizes recall by aggregating results from different approaches

RAGParentDocument.label: Parent Document RAG
RAGParentDocument.description: Best for: Precise retrieval with comprehensive surrounding context. • Searches using small child chunks for accuracy • Returns larger parent documents for complete context • Balances retrieval precision with generation context needs

RAGStepBack.label: Step-Back RAG
RAGStepBack.description: Best for: Questions needing high-level conceptual understanding first. • Generates broader 'step-back' questions from specific queries • Retrieves foundational concepts before specific details • Improves reasoning by establishing conceptual framework

RAGDecomposition.label: Decomposition RAG
RAGDecomposition.description: Best for: Complex multi-part questions requiring step-by-step analysis. • Breaks questions into 2-3 simpler sub-questions • Processes each sub-question independently in parallel • Combines results addressing different aspects of complex queries

RAGGraphSearch.label: Graph Search RAG
RAGGraphSearch.description: Best for: Queries about entity relationships and knowledge graph traversal. • Uses Neo4j graph database for relationship-aware retrieval • Performs multi-hop reasoning across connected entities • Combines vector similarity with graph structure understanding

RAGGenerativeFeedbackLoop.label: Generative Feedback Loop
RAGGenerativeFeedbackLoop.description: Best for: Complex questions where initial retrieval needs refinement. • Evaluates answer quality and identifies information gaps • Iteratively refines queries based on generation feedback • Self-corrects by retrieving additional context when needed

RAGQueryChain.label: Query Chain RAG
RAGQueryChain.description: Best for: Sequential query processing with progressive refinement. • Chains multiple query transformation steps • Each step refines the query based on previous results • Enables complex multi-stage retrieval workflows

RAGRewriteRetrieveRead.label: Rewrite-Retrieve-Read
RAGRewriteRetrieveRead.description: Best for: Poorly formulated queries needing optimization for search. • Rewrites user queries into retrieval-optimized versions • Uses LLM to transform natural questions into search-friendly terms • Maintains original intent while improving document matching

RAGAgenticExpansion.label: Agentic Expansion
RAGAgenticExpansion.description: Best for: Complex queries needing intelligent context navigation. • Uses AI agent to analyze document structure • Intelligently selects relevant sections beyond vector search • Adaptive expansion based on query complexity • Optimal balance of context richness and computational efficiency

RAGAdaptiveOrchestrator.label: Adaptive - Smart Strategy Selection
RAGAdaptiveOrchestrator.description: Dynamically selects techniques based on query type (metadata, semantic, knowledge-intensive, or contextual). Combines HybridSelfQuery, MultiQuery, HyDE and more based on query analysis. Analyzes keywords and patterns to determine the best approach for each query.

RAGPrecisionOrchestrator.label: Precision - Maximum Accuracy
RAGPrecisionOrchestrator.description: Combines HybridSelfQuery for metadata filtering with MultiQuery to generate query variations. Applies document reranking to ensure highest relevance. Optimizes for accuracy by using multiple retrieval techniques in parallel with result fusion.

RAGBalancedOrchestrator.label: Balanced - Speed & Accuracy
RAGBalancedOrchestrator.description: Provides a good balance between accuracy and performance. Uses a mix of metadata filtering and semantic search with optimized document count and selective reranking. Best compromise for general-purpose use cases.

RAGContextEnhancedOrchestrator.label: Context Enhanced - Conversation Aware
RAGContextEnhancedOrchestrator.description: Leverages conversation history to improve retrieval by tracking key entities and context over time. Adapts retrieval strategy based on conversational context and generates context-aware queries. Best for multi-turn conversations with evolving topics.

RAGKnowledgeIntensiveOrchestrator.label: Knowledge Intensive - Complex Reasoning
RAGKnowledgeIntensiveOrchestrator.description: Combines StepBack reasoning to identify broader concepts with Query Decomposition to break down complex questions. Uses Langchain RunnablePassthrough for document processing and weights results based on query complexity. Best for complex 'why' questions and multi-step reasoning.

RAGDocumentHierarchyOrchestrator.label: Document Hierarchy - Structure Aware
RAGDocumentHierarchyOrchestrator.description: Leverages document structure and parent-child relationships in your content. Uses query rewriting to capture document hierarchy and parent document context. Particularly effective for structured documents like manuals and hierarchical knowledge bases.

RAGQueryRefinementOrchestrator.label: Query Refinement - Ambiguity Handler
RAGQueryRefinementOrchestrator.description: Uses query refinement chains to handle ambiguous and underspecified queries. Combines query expansion, reformulation, and fusion techniques to generate improved queries. Effective for exploratory search and ambiguous topics requiring clarification.

RAGFeedbackLoopOrchestrator.label: Feedback Loop - Self-Correcting
RAGFeedbackLoopOrchestrator.description: Implements iterative refinement through generative feedback loops. Evaluates retrieval quality and adapts based on results, enabling self-correction. Uses answer relevance checks and query reformulation to handle retrieval failures and edge cases.

EnhancedTriModalOrchestrator.label: Enhanced Tri-Modal - Advanced Fusion
EnhancedTriModalOrchestrator.description: Advanced tri-modal fusion combining dense, sparse, and graph-based retrieval with enhanced scoring and cross-modal verification. Provides the best overall quality for complex queries requiring multi-source evidence.

documents.title: Document Sources
documents.description: Configure embedding models and document chunking strategies

embeddingModel.tooltip: Embedding models convert text into vectors for similarity searching. Different models offer various trade-offs between accuracy, speed, and language support.
embeddingModel.label: Model

splitterType.label: Document Splitting Strategy
splitterType.semanticDescription: Analyzes content for natural semantic breakpoints, producing more coherent chunks.
splitterType.recursiveDescription: Split documents into chunks of predefined size with overlap, ignoring document structure.
splitterType.enhanced_markdownDescription: Advanced markdown processing with metadata extraction and rich formatting support.
splitterType.propositionDescription: Splits text into atomic, factual propositions for knowledge extraction and fact-based retrieval.
splitterType.hierarchicalDescription: Preserves document hierarchy and nested structure (chapters, sections, subsections).
splitterType.topic_basedDescription: Groups content by thematic coherence using topic modeling (LDA, clustering).
splitterType.sliding_windowDescription: Creates overlapping chunks with configurable overlap for enhanced context preservation.
splitterType.agenticDescription: Uses LLM-powered intelligent analysis for boundary detection and proposition grouping.
splitterType.concept_awareDescription: Preserves domain-specific terminology, concepts, and their relationships.
splitterType.narrative_structureDescription: Maintains story flow, temporal sequences, and narrative coherence.
splitterType.token_basedDescription: Precise token-aware chunking using various tokenizers (tiktoken, HuggingFace).
splitterType.gpt_tokenDescription: Optimized token chunking for OpenAI GPT models with precise token control.
splitterType.claude_tokenDescription: Optimized token chunking for Anthropic Claude models with precise token control.
splitterType.openai_semanticDescription: Semantic chunking optimized for OpenAI embeddings with high-quality results.
splitterType.huggingface_semanticDescription: Semantic chunking using HuggingFace embedding models for open-source solutions.
splitterType.document_structureDescription: Auto-detects document type and uses appropriate structure-aware splitting.
splitterType.langchain_markdownDescription: Advanced Markdown chunking preserving headers and hierarchical structure.
splitterType.htmlDescription: HTML-aware chunking preserving tags and semantic structure for web content.
splitterType.codeDescription: Language-aware code chunking preserving functions and classes for source code.
splitterType.placeholder: Select a chunking strategy to see its description.

chunkingOptions.title: Document Chunking Strategy
chunkingOptions.description: How documents are split affects retrieval quality. Smaller chunks are more precise but may miss context; larger chunks provide more context but may introduce noise.
chunkingOptions.recommended: For most use cases, we recommend a balance between chunk size and overlap.
chunkingOptions.bestBalance: Best balance of precision and context
chunkingOptions.betterContextMayIncludeIrrelevantInfo: Better context, may include irrelevant info
chunkingOptions.maximumContextPotentialNoise: Maximum context, higher potential for noise
chunkingOptions.customize: You can customize these settings to fit your specific documents and use cases.

ocrEnabled.description: When enabled, uses Docling for layout-aware extraction (detects headers, titles, quotes). When disabled, uses PyMuPDF4LLM for faster text-only extraction.

tooltip.title: Choose how to split your documents into chunks:
tooltip.recursive: Best for most documents, preserves structure

chunkSize.label: Chunk Size (tokens)
chunkSize.tooltip: The size of each document chunk in tokens. Smaller chunks (128-256) are better for precise retrieval but may miss context. Larger chunks (512-1024) capture more context but may be less precise.
chunkSize.recommended: 256 tokens recommended for most use cases

chunkOverlap.label: Chunk Overlap (tokens)
chunkOverlap.tooltip: The number of overlapping tokens between adjacent chunks. This helps maintain context across chunk boundaries. A good rule of thumb is ~20% of chunk size.

chunkSizesToIgnore.label: Chunk Sizes To Ignore
chunkSizesToIgnore.tooltip: Minimum chunk size in tokens. Chunks smaller than this will be ignored during processing. This helps filter out very small, potentially irrelevant text fragments.

aiAssistant.title: AI Assistant
aiAssistant.content:
You are a helpful, polite, and honest assistant. Always respond as helpfully as possible, while being safe. Your responses should not include any harmful, unethical, racist, sexist, toxic, dangerous, or illegal content. Please ensure that your responses are socially unbiased and positive in nature.

If a question is not clear or is factually coherent, explain why instead of answering something not correct. If you don't know the answer to a question, please don't share false information.

codeExpert.title: Code Expert
codeExpert.content: You are an experienced programmer with expertise in multiple programming languages. Help the user write, debug, and optimize code. Provide clear explanations and follow best practices for the programming language being used.

tooltip.maxOutputTokens: Maximum number of tokens the model can generate in a single response
tooltip.temperature: Controls randomness: lower values are more focused, higher values are more creative
tooltip.contextWindowSize: Maximum context size in tokens the model can process
tooltip.topP: Nucleus sampling: only consider tokens within the top P probability mass
tooltip.topK: Only sample from the top K most likely tokens at each step
tooltip.frequencyPenalty: Penalizes tokens based on how frequently they appear, reducing repetition
tooltip.presencePenalty: Penalizes tokens that have already appeared, encouraging topic diversity
tooltip.gpuLayers: Number of model layers to offload to GPU (-1 = all layers)
tooltip.contextMessageLimit: Maximum number of previous messages included in context
tooltip.extraModelParameters: Additional JSON parameters passed directly to the model API

augmentation.title: Knowledge augmentation
augmentation.description: Whether the AI may add general knowledge alongside cited sources.
augmentation.augmentedHint: Always add a clearly-labeled wider-context paragraph
augmentation.autoHint: Add wider context only when retrieval is incomplete

fileAttachment.mediaUploadHint: Any audio/video — transcribed for this message (not just YouTube). Max 25MB; larger files go to a Knowledge collection.
fileAttachment.mediaSizeLimit: File too large for inline transcription (max 25MB). Upload it to a Knowledge collection instead.
fileAttachment.visionModelNeeded: Requires a vision-capable model (e.g. GPT-4o, Claude 3)
fileAttachment.dragAndDrop: Drag and drop or click to browse
fileAttachment.youtubeDescription: Paste a YouTube URL to fetch its transcript and include it in the conversation context.

fileAttachment.fetchFailed: Could not fetch transcript automatically. You can paste it manually below.

fileAttachment.youtubeNotSupported: Playlist and channel transcript extraction is not yet supported. Please paste individual video URLs.

license.title: License Agreement
license.agreeTerms: I agree to the terms and conditions of the Scrapalot license agreement
license.agreementIntro: By using Scrapalot, you agree to use the software responsibly and in accordance with applicable laws.
license.permittedUsesDesc: Research, data analysis, content aggregation for personal or commercial purposes.
license.prohibitedUsesDesc: Illegal activities, harassment, spam, or violation of third-party rights.
license.dataPrivacyDesc: Your data is stored locally and securely. We do not sell or share your personal information.
license.disclaimerDesc: The software is provided "as is" without warranties. Use at your own risk.
license.shareUsageData: Share anonymous usage data to help us improve the application (optional)
license.whatWeCollectDesc: If you opt in, we collect anonymous usage statistics such as feature usage, error reports, and performance metrics.
license.whatWeDontCollectDesc: Personal data, conversation content, or any identifiable information.
license.purposeDesc: To understand how users interact with Scrapalot and identify areas for improvement.
license.changeInSettings: You can change this preference anytime in Settings.

saveFailed.title: Failed to save
saveFailed.description: Could not save page metadata. Please retry.

ttsEmpty.title: Nothing to read
ttsEmpty.description: Add some content to the note first.

reactionNotSaved.title: Save the note first
reactionNotSaved.description: Type at least a title — the note has to exist before you can react.

descriptions.generateTitle: Reads the note and inserts a concise H1 title at the top. Prerequisite: at least a short paragraph of body text — the title is derived from existing content.
descriptions.generateAbstract: Summarises the note into a short abstract (blockquote) inserted just below the title. Prerequisite: a draft of at least a few paragraphs.
descriptions.generateOutline: Opens a template picker (IMRaD, literature review, etc.) and appends a structured outline based on your note. Prerequisite: at least 50 characters of content.
descriptions.generateHighlights: Extracts the most important points of the note into a "Key highlights" section appended at the end. Prerequisite: a draft to summarise from.
descriptions.composeFromSources: Opens a dialog to draft a new section grounded in selected research sources. Prerequisite: pick a research context (collections / documents) so the model has sources to cite.
descriptions.connectDots: Surfaces non-obvious links between claims in the note and your research sources. Prerequisite: an active research context and a draft with at least a few claims.
descriptions.factCheckWholeNote: Verifies the factual claims in the note against your research sources and returns supporting / contradicting evidence. Prerequisite: a research context with sources.
descriptions.critiqueWithQuestions: Generates 3–5 critical questions about your draft as a "review" callout at the end of the note. Prerequisite: at least 50 characters of content.
descriptions.shortenToTarget: Opens a dialog where you choose a target word count; rewrites the note shorter while keeping its core meaning. Prerequisite: a draft long enough that shortening makes sense.
descriptions.translateWholeNote: Replaces the note content with a translation between English and Croatian (auto-picks the opposite of your current UI language). Prerequisite: at least 20 characters of content.

outlineEmpty.title: Note too short
outlineEmpty.description: Add at least 50 characters before generating an outline.

outlineDone.description: Outline appended to the end of the note.

knowledgeGaps.title: Knowledge Gaps
knowledgeGaps.subtitle: Detect orphan nodes, dead ends, and low-density areas in the knowledge graph
knowledgeGaps.orphanEntitiesDesc: Extracted but not linked to any document chunk
knowledgeGaps.deadEndEntitiesDesc: Only 1 co-occurrence connection — isolated in the graph
knowledgeGaps.isolatedBooksDesc: No shared entities with other books — knowledge island
knowledgeGaps.lowDensitySectionsDesc: Sections with chunks but no entities extracted
knowledgeGaps.crossDocLonelyDesc: Appear in 3+ documents but never co-occur with other entities
knowledgeGaps.noIssues: No knowledge gaps detected — graph is healthy
knowledgeGaps.reprocessConfirm: Re-extract entities for "{{label}}"? This re-runs the entity pipeline (~30–60s per document) and may briefly increase load.

documents.enterIdToStart: Select a collection to begin inspection.

empty.title: Document Inspector
empty.description: Select a collection or document from the sidebar to begin inspecting quality metrics.
empty.hint: Click a collection to expand, then select a document

processing.noData: No processing statistics available for this document.

documents.reprocessConfirm: Re-chunk and re-embed this document with the latest pipeline. Existing entities will be preserved.
documents.reprocessing: Reprocessing document... This may take a few minutes. The view will refresh automatically when complete.

graph.selectScope: Select a scope and load the graph to visualize.
graph.emptyGraphDesc: This document has not been indexed into the knowledge graph. Try reprocessing it with graph extraction enabled.
graph.processingCompleteDesc: Document has been reprocessed. Graph data is now available.
graph.processingFailedDesc: Failed to reprocess document for graph extraction.

crossBook.selectToStart: Select a collection to explore cross-book entity relationships.
crossBook.selectToStartHint: The graph visualizes shared entities between books, showing how documents in a collection are connected through common people, places, concepts, and other entities.
crossBook.noRelationshipsHint: Try lowering the minimum shared entities threshold or rebuild the graph.
crossBook.rebuildHint: Click "Rebuild graph" to analyze entity relationships across all books in this collection. This may take a few minutes depending on the number of documents.

rag.noTracesDesc: RAG traces appear here after users send chat messages. Traces show routing decisions, latency, tokens, and confidence scores.
rag.cleanupOrphaned: Delete traces for sessions that no longer exist
rag.cleanupSuccess: Cleaned up: {{rag}} RAG traces, {{llm}} LLM traces
rag.expansionDeferredNotice: Per-anchor expansion telemetry (IDF rarity, hop depth, budget rejections) is not yet persisted. This view shows graph traversal stats for traces that exercised a graph-using strategy.
rag.expansionEmpty: No graph-expansion traces in the current window.

errors.timeout: Polling timed out — check Data Inspector later.

edgeInspector.title: Edge inspector
edgeInspector.noSourceChunks: No source chunks recorded — edge predates per-edge provenance tracking.

llm.noLLMTracesDesc: LLM traces appear here after users send chat messages. Traces show provider, model, tokens, cost, and latency for each request.
llm.contextTokensUsed: context tokens used for RAG retrieval
llm.systemPromptNotAvailable: System prompt not available in preview

costs.title: LLM Cost Analysis
costs.description: Track and analyze LLM token usage and costs across all system agents.
costs.noData: No cost data available yet. Costs appear after LLM calls are tracked.

evaluation.title: Graph Quality Audit
evaluation.description: Real-time integrity check comparing Neo4j knowledge graph against PostgreSQL source data.
evaluation.coverageDesc: Documents in PostgreSQL vs Book nodes in Neo4j
evaluation.relationshipDesc: CO_OCCURS_WITH validity (both entities must have MENTIONS)
evaluation.chunkAlignmentDesc: Neo4j Chunk nodes matched to PG embeddings (sampled)
evaluation.noData: No graph data available. Run a graph rebuild first.
evaluation.crossBookDesc: SHARED_ENTITY relationships between books sharing entities

views.extractionDesc: Entity extraction phase metrics and cost
views.housekeepingDesc: Audit and clean the knowledge graph

extraction.title: Entity Extraction Costs
extraction.subtitle: spaCy / LLM / Neo4j extraction phase aggregates over the chosen window
extraction.llmSkippedHint: Chunks the spaCy NER pre-filter handled without an LLM call
extraction.empty: No extraction runs recorded in this window
extraction.noDocuments: No documents extracted in this window

communities.title: Leiden Communities
communities.empty: No communities yet — click 'Build communities' to run hierarchical Leiden + LLM reports.
communities.selectACommunity: Select a community to view its report.
communities.noReport: No report stored for this community yet.

housekeeping.title: Graph Housekeeping
housekeeping.subtitle: Audit and clean the Neo4j knowledge graph. Read-only health checks are always safe; destructive ops support a dry-run preview.

healthCheck.title: Graph Health Check
healthCheck.description: Read-only audit of orphans, duplicates, chapter detection, and schema integrity.
healthCheck.clean: No issues found. Graph is healthy.

sweep.title: Sweep Orphan Nodes
sweep.description: Delete Chapter / Section / Chunk / Book nodes that have no parent in the hierarchy.
sweep.confirm: Permanently delete orphan nodes? This cannot be undone.

merge.title: Merge Duplicate Entities
merge.description: Collapse Entity nodes sharing the same canonical name into a single multi-labeled node.
merge.confirm: Merge duplicate entities across the entire graph? This rewrites Neo4j.

cleanup.title: Cleanup Missing-File Documents
cleanup.description: Mark documents whose source file is gone as failed and delete embeddings + Neo4j hierarchy.
cleanup.confirm: Permanently delete embeddings and graph nodes for documents whose source file is missing? This cannot be undone.

mergePreview.sample: Sample of duplicate groups that would merge

sessionFolders.createFolderDescription: Organize your conversations into a named folder.
sessionFolders.editFolderDescription: Change the name of this folder.
sessionFolders.moveToFolderDescription: Choose a destination folder for this conversation.
sessionFolders.deleted: Folder deleted. Sessions returned to Unfiled.
sessionFolders.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete the folder "{{name}}"? All conversations in this folder will be moved to Unfiled.
sessionFolders.createFolderFirst: Create a folder first to move sessions into it.
sessionFolders.dragToMove: Drag to move to another folder

dm.title: Messages
dm.noMembers: No other members in this workspace
dm.startTyping: Send a message to start the conversation

workspaceChat.clearConfirm: Delete the entire conversation? This cannot be undone.
workspaceChat.rollbackConfirm: Roll the conversation back to this point? Every message from here onward will be deleted.

share.title: Share Conversation
share.description: Anyone with the link can view this conversation
share.noActiveShare: No active share link for this conversation.
share.notFound: This shared conversation is no longer available

paper.title: Generate Paper
paper.description: Create a formatted paper from your research and notes.
paper.templateLockedByVenue: Template is set by the selected venue.
paper.noSources: No sources selected. Paper will be generated from template structure only.
paper.sectionNote: Drag to reorder. Each section builds on the previous ones.
paper.selectTemplate: Select a template to preview sections.
paper.generateError: Failed to generate paper. Please try again.

templateGallery.title: Choose a template
templateGallery.description: Each template seeds a structure and a suggested research scope. All editable after creation.
templateGallery.tipFooter: Tip: save any note as a custom template via File → Save as new template.

academic-imrad.name: Academic article (IMRaD)
academic-imrad.description: Introduction, Methods, Results, Discussion. Formal scholarly tone with inline citations.
academic-imrad.skeleton:
# [Title — 60–120 characters]

*[Abstract — 150–250 words covering motivation, method, key finding, implication.]*

## Introduction

*[Background, the gap in the literature, the research question, and what this paper contributes — 2–3 paragraphs.]*

## Methods

*[Data sources, analysis approach, tools used. Be reproducible.]*

## Results

*[Findings with inline citations. Lead with the headline result.]*

## Discussion

*[Interpretation, limits, future work, implications for practice.]*

## References

peer-review.name: Peer review report
peer-review.description: Summary · Strengths · Weaknesses · Recommendations. Constructive, rigorous tone.
peer-review.skeleton:
# Peer review: [Manuscript title]

**Reviewer:** [Your name]
**Recommendation:** [Accept / Minor revisions / Major revisions / Reject]

## Summary

*[1–2 paragraphs restating what the paper does in your own words.]*

## Strengths

- *[Concrete strength with reference to a section]*
- *[…]*

## Weaknesses

- *[Concrete weakness with reference to a section]*
- *[…]*

## Specific comments

1. *[Page / line — issue + suggestion]*
2. *[…]*

## Recommendations

*[Top 3 changes that would meaningfully improve the manuscript.]*

literature-review.name: Literature review
literature-review.description: Background, current state, gaps, synthesis across the field.
literature-review.skeleton:
# Literature review: [Topic]

## Scope

*[What you are reviewing and why. Include the inclusion / exclusion criteria.]*

## Background

*[Foundational works the rest of the literature builds on.]*

## Current state

*[Recent (last 5 years) findings, organized thematically — not chronologically.]*

## Gaps and tensions

*[Where the literature disagrees, what remains untested, where methodology is thin.]*

## Synthesis

*[Your own integrative framing. Don't just list — connect.]*

## References

research-proposal.name: Research proposal
research-proposal.description: Problem · significance · approach · timeline. For grant or thesis intake.
research-proposal.skeleton:
# Research proposal: [Title]

## Problem statement

*[The problem in 1 paragraph. Why it matters — to whom.]*

## Significance

*[What changes if you succeed. Who benefits.]*

## Background and prior work

*[3–5 most relevant references with one-sentence summaries.]*

## Research questions

1. *[Specific, falsifiable question]*
2. *[…]*

## Approach

*[Methodology, datasets, instruments. Be specific enough that another researcher could replicate.]*

## Timeline

| Month | Milestone |
|---|---|
| 1–2 | *[…]* |
| 3–6 | *[…]* |
| 7–12 | *[…]* |

## Expected outcomes

*[Concrete deliverables — papers, software, datasets.]*

## References

medium-article.name: Medium article
medium-article.skeleton:
# [Hook headline — make a reader stop scrolling]

*[Subtitle — one sentence promise of what the reader gets.]*

[Opening paragraph — start with a vivid scene, a startling statistic, or a contrarian claim. Earn the next paragraph.]

## [First section heading]

[Body. Short paragraphs. One idea per paragraph.]

> [Pull-quote — the most quotable line.]

## [Second section heading]

[…]

## [Third section heading]

[…]

## What to take away

[3 bullet points the reader should remember tomorrow.]

— *[Your name]*

blog-post.name: Blog post
blog-post.description: Conversational 5–6 paragraphs, social-friendly TL;DR at the end.
blog-post.skeleton:
# [Headline]

[Hook — what made you write this today?]

[Context — the problem or observation you're addressing.]

[Heart of the post — your insight, story, or argument.]

[Implication — what the reader should think or do differently.]

[Close — a question, a call to action, or a memorable image.]

---

**TL;DR:** *[One sentence the reader can copy-paste into a tweet.]*

philosophical-essay.name: Philosophical essay
philosophical-essay.description: Thesis → argument → counterexample → synthesis. Bridge-mode friendly.
philosophical-essay.skeleton:
# [Question or claim as the title]

## Thesis

*[State the position you will defend in 1–2 sentences.]*

## The argument

*[Build the case. Define key terms. Cite traditions or thinkers where relevant.]*

## Strongest objection

*[Steel-man the position you disagree with. Treat it generously.]*

## Reply

*[Show why your position survives the objection — or refine the thesis.]*

## Implications

*[What follows if the thesis holds. For practice, for other questions, for self-understanding.]*

## Sources consulted

social-thread.name: Social media thread
social-thread.description: Numbered 8–12 post sequence. Hook, body, payoff.
social-thread.skeleton:
# Thread: [Topic]

**1/** *[Hook — make people stop. Concrete, specific, contrarian, or vulnerable.]*

**2/** *[Set the stakes — why this matters to the reader right now.]*

**3/** *[First insight or beat of the story.]*

**4/** *[Second beat — escalate.]*

**5/** *[Third beat — reveal the hidden mechanism / surprise.]*

**6/** *[Practical takeaway #1.]*

**7/** *[Practical takeaway #2.]*

**8/** *[Synthesis — what does it all mean.]*

**9/** *[Soft CTA — follow / save / share, or a question to invite replies.]*

personal-journal.name: Personal journal
personal-journal.description: First-person reflective. No citations, no research. Private by default.
personal-journal.skeleton:
# [Date or mood as the title]

[What happened today — outwardly.]

[How I noticed it inwardly — what I felt, what surprised me.]

[What I'm carrying forward — a question, a lesson, a gentle recognition.]

reading-notes.name: Reading notes
reading-notes.description: Per-chapter or per-section capture. Quotes + my reaction + open questions.
reading-notes.skeleton:
# [Source title] — reading notes

**Author:** [Author]
**Section / chapter:** [Where I am]

## Key passages

> *[Quote, with page number]*

> *[Quote, with page number]*

## My reaction

*[What this provoked in me. Where it agrees with or contradicts something I've thought.]*

## Open questions

- *[Question the author raises but doesn't fully answer]*
- *[Something I want to look up next]*

## Connect to

*[Other books / ideas this links to.]*

bugTracker.title: Bug Tracker
bugTracker.submitDisabledHint: Report Bug is enabled only when the active tab's logs contain an ERROR.
